skill.name: conductor-revert
skill.description: Git-aware undo by logical work unit (track, phase, or task)
skill.summary: Safely revert git changes by logical units with conflict detection and explicit confirmation
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 59 static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are Markdown documentation examples, not executable code. The skill describes safe git revert operations with explicit safety rules forbidding dangerous commands like git reset --hard and git push --force. Requires explicit user confirmation before any changes.

content.user_title: Revert git changes by logical work unit
content.value_statement: Safely undo git commits by track, phase, or task with full history preservation. This skill provides structured revert workflows with explicit confirmation and conflict detection.
actual_capabilities: Revert entire git tracks, specific phases, or individual tasks
Detect and report merge conflicts before they occur
Display full execution plan with affected files before any changes
Update plan.md and track status after successful reverts
Handle edge cases like already-reverted commits or unpushed changes
Require explicit YES confirmation before executing any git commands
limitations: Requires git repository with Conductor tracks initialized
Cannot revert commits that do not follow the expected naming format
Requires manual intervention for merge conflicts
Only works with commits that reference track identifiers in messages

use_cases.title: Undo a failed feature implementation
use_cases.description: Revert all commits for a specific task that introduced bugs or did not meet requirements
use_cases.target_user: Developers who need to safely roll back problematic changes

use_cases.title: Restore previous working state
use_cases.description: Revert an entire phase of work to return to a known good state before a major refactor
use_cases.target_user: Team leads managing iterative development workflows

use_cases.title: Clean up experimental work
use_cases.description: Remove all traces of an experimental track that was not merged to production
use_cases.target_user: Engineers who prototype features that may be abandoned

prompt_templates.title: Revert a specific task
prompt_templates.prompt: Revert task 2.3 in the auth_20250115 track. Show me what commits will be affected and confirm before proceeding.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to undo a specific task that introduced a bug

prompt_templates.title: Revert an entire phase
prompt_templates.prompt: Revert phase 2 of the dashboard_20250112 track. I want to see the full plan with all affected files before confirming.
prompt_templates.scenario: An entire phase of work needs to be rolled back

prompt_templates.title: Interactive revert selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me revert some recent work. Show me the available tracks and tasks I can revert.
prompt_templates.scenario: You are unsure which specific changes to revert

prompt_templates.title: Full track removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Revert the entire experiment_20250120 track. This experimental feature was not approved and needs to be fully removed from history.
prompt_templates.scenario: Removing all traces of an abandoned experimental feature

output_examples.output:

Commits to revert (in reverse chronological order):

  2. def5678 - chore: mark task 2.3 complete (auth_20250115)

Files that will be affected:
  - src/auth/oauth.ts (will be deleted)
  - src/routes/callback.ts (will be deleted)
  - conductor/tracks/auth_20250115/plan.md (modified)

Plan updates:
  - Task 2.3: [~] -> [ ]

!! WARNING !!
This operation will:
- Create 2 revert commits
- Modify 3 files
- Reset 1 task to pending status

Type 'YES' to proceed, or anything else to cancel:

Target: Phase 1 in dashboard_20250112 (Tasks 1.1 through 1.4)

Commits to revert (in reverse chronological order):

Type 'YES' to proceed, or anything else to cancel:

best_practices: Always review the full execution plan before confirming any revert operation
Run tests after revert completion to verify the application works correctly
Communicate with your team before reverting shared or reviewed work
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to hide mistakes from team members - discuss issues openly
Avoid reverting work that others have built upon without coordination
Do not skip the explicit confirmation step - always review the plan first

faq.question: What is the difference between git revert and git reset?
faq.answer: Git revert creates new commits that undo previous changes while preserving history. Git reset removes commits entirely, rewriting history. This skill uses only git revert for safety and audit trails.

faq.question: Can I revert commits that have already been pushed to remote?
faq.answer: Yes. Reverting pushed commits creates new revert commits that you can safely push. This is the recommended approach over force pushing, which can cause problems for collaborators.

faq.question: What happens if a merge conflict occurs during revert?
faq.answer: The skill halts immediately and displays conflict details. You must manually resolve conflicts before continuing. The skill preserves completed reverts and provides guidance for resolution.

faq.question: Can I undo a revert if I made a mistake?
faq.answer: Yes. You can run git revert on the revert commits to restore the original changes. The skill provides undo instructions in the verification output.

faq.question: What if the skill cannot find commits for my track?
faq.answer: This may mean the track was never committed, or commits used a different naming format. The skill will offer to delete the track directory or cancel the operation.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my plan.md automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. After successful git reverts, the skill updates plan.md to mark reverted tasks as pending. These changes are not committed - they reflect the new state of your work.
